evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: GAMMA aims to retrofit an Ultramax bulk carrier and demonstrate safe integration of biomethanol, ammonia and fuel-cell related systems for emission reduction in commercial vessels.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: The project goal is retrofitting an Ultramax bulk carrier for international routes and testing biomethanol/NH3 fuel systems.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Strong project-level record with vessel retrofit, demonstration, alternative fuels and fuel value-chain testing.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Alternative Fuels and Green Shipping within maritime R&D.
evaluations.possibleApplication: Potentially applicable to maritime operators and researchers working on Alternative Fuels and Green Shipping, pending review of full project outcomes.
evaluations.whyItMatters: Strong project-level record with vessel retrofit, demonstration, alternative fuels and fuel value-chain testing.
evaluations.limitations: Based on project metadata, summary and coordinator-page evidence gathered so far; full technical publications and outcome reports have not been reviewed.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: DYNAPORT develops optimisation and coordination tools for ports and ships to reduce fuel consumption and improve port efficiency by at least 10%.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: The project aims to reduce fuel consumption and enhance port efficiency by at least 10%.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Directly aligned with smart ports, emissions reduction and operational optimisation.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Smart Ports and Green Shipping within maritime R&D.
evaluations.possibleApplication: Potentially applicable to maritime operators and researchers working on Smart Ports and Green Shipping, pending review of full project outcomes.
evaluations.whyItMatters: Directly aligned with smart ports, emissions reduction and operational optimisation.
evaluations.limitations: Based on project metadata, summary and coordinator-page evidence gathered so far; full technical publications and outcome reports have not been reviewed.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: TwinShip develops an open-source digital platform and digital-twin enabled decision support system for green ship operations and emission reduction.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: The platform is powered by domain knowledge-based machine learning and AI capabilities and targets major GHG reductions.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Combines digital twin, AI/ML, clean fuels and green ship operations; suitable for country and technology-area panels.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Green Shipping and Maritime AI / Digital Twin within maritime R&D.
evaluations.possibleApplication: Potentially applicable to maritime operators and researchers working on Green Shipping and Maritime AI / Digital Twin, pending review of full project outcomes.
evaluations.whyItMatters: Combines digital twin, AI/ML, clean fuels and green ship operations; suitable for country and technology-area panels.
evaluations.limitations: Based on project metadata, summary and coordinator-page evidence gathered so far; full technical publications and outcome reports have not been reviewed.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: SAFeCRAFT demonstrates sustainable alternative fuel technologies, including hydrogen, ammonia and LOHC-based options, for waterborne transport applications.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: The project demonstrates four SAF-enabling technologies while utilising H2 on a demonstration vessel.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Very strong practical R&D record with demo vessel, safety assessment, energy efficiency and techno-economic feasibility.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Alternative Fuels and Green Shipping within maritime R&D.
evaluations.possibleApplication: Potentially applicable to maritime operators and researchers working on Alternative Fuels and Green Shipping, pending review of full project outcomes.
evaluations.whyItMatters: Very strong practical R&D record with demo vessel, safety assessment, energy efficiency and techno-economic feasibility.
evaluations.limitations: Based on project metadata, summary and coordinator-page evidence gathered so far; full technical publications and outcome reports have not been reviewed.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: MERLIN deploys hydrogen-powered unmanned surface vessels and autonomous underwater vessels operating autonomously using AI algorithms.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: MERLIN uses hydrogen fuel cells and renewable energy for USV/AUV autonomy with AI algorithms.
evaluations.technologyApproach: High-value autonomous marine robotics project with AI and hydrogen-powered vehicle signal.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Autonomous Vessels and Maritime AI / Marine Robotics within maritime R&D.
evaluations.possibleApplication: Potentially applicable to maritime operators and researchers working on Autonomous Vessels and Maritime AI / Marine Robotics, pending review of full project outcomes.
evaluations.whyItMatters: High-value autonomous marine robotics project with AI and hydrogen-powered vehicle signal.
evaluations.limitations: Based on project metadata, summary and coordinator-page evidence gathered so far; full technical publications and outcome reports have not been reviewed.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: AUTOFLEX develops small autonomous zero-emission vessels for inland waterway freight, focusing on narrow and shallow waterways.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: The project develops small, autonomous, zero-emission vessels optimized for energy efficiency and shallow waterways.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Clear autonomous vessel and zero-emission inland shipping application.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Autonomous Vessels and Green Shipping within maritime R&D.
evaluations.possibleApplication: Potentially applicable to maritime operators and researchers working on Autonomous Vessels and Green Shipping, pending review of full project outcomes.
evaluations.whyItMatters: Clear autonomous vessel and zero-emission inland shipping application.
evaluations.limitations: Based on project metadata, summary and coordinator-page evidence gathered so far; full technical publications and outcome reports have not been reviewed.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: DT4GS supports shipping companies in reducing CO2e through real-time configurable digital twins for ship and fleet operational performance optimisation.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: The project aims to support up to 20% CO2e reduction by deploying digital twins in Living Labs.
evaluations.technologyApproach: One of the clearest green-shipping digital twin projects; useful for AI/digitalisation country profiles.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Green Shipping and Maritime AI / Digital Twin within maritime R&D.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: MISSION focuses on interoperable port call and voyage optimisation tools for maritime just-in-time operations.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: MISSION develops a digital real-time optimisation and decision-support tool for port call operations.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Useful for smart port and voyage optimisation category, especially operational decarbonisation.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Smart Ports and Maritime Logistics within maritime R&D.

evaluations.plainLanguageExplanation: SEAMLESS focuses on safe, efficient and autonomous solutions for European short sea and inland waterway transport.
evaluations.problemBeingAddressed: SEAMLESS develops automated, cost-effective and resilient waterborne freight feeder services.
evaluations.technologyApproach: Relevant to autonomous waterborne transport and multimodal logistics.
evaluations.maritimeRelevance: Directly relevant to Autonomous Vessels and Maritime Logistics within maritime R&D.
